title: Test A733 SMHC0 AHB gate at CCU offset 0x5dc
observations: The lab kernel attempted to set CCU offset 0x5dc bit 5 before normal IDMAC launch.
The write did not stick: before=0x00000000 and after=0x00000000.
The fabric dump continued to show ahb_smhc=0x00000000.
Normal IDMAC still entered DESC_READ with IDST=0x00004000, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x00000400, BBCR=0x00000000.
The descriptor remained unchanged after CPU sync, with OWN still set.
conclusion: The 0x5dc bit5 SMHC0 AHB gate lead does not currently provide a Linux-side fix. It is either not a writable/live register in this context, is protected/owned by firmware, or the generated U-Boot header name does not map to a usable SDMMC0 IDMAC precondition.
next_action: Do not repeat on Cubie2 unless a new reason appears. Continue with source-backed firmware/bootloader comparison or maintainer/vendor feedback for the missing SDMMC0 IDMAC descriptor-fetch precondition.
